binding: Scenario B (count-based n_eff), HoI-ratified 13 Jul 2026 — see docs/20260713_INT_BRIEF_PCS-CI-Neff-ScenarioB-Ratification_v01.md. ANCHORS mirror the watch cases of tests/golden/pcs-golden-v21.json (Method v21 §12), asserted at the watches scaleFactor (10). They prove the deterministic engine reproduces known watch tiers to the digit. FIELD_GOLDEN are objects run end-to-end through watches.v1.json → enrich → score, each with an expert-assigned ground-truth tier; a category is only honestly 'calibrated' once its field_golden set validates. SOFT-LAUNCH BASIS: the field set is built from documented reference models (Rolex Submariner 16610; a known super-clone) with expert-known market positioning — full-set genuine → Gold, no-papers genuine → Silver, super-clone → Flagged. Physical owner objects and Rapp/Christie's auction lots deepen the set as they land; the negative (super-clone) is present per Acceptance B.

field_golden.name: Rolex Submariner 16610 — full set, single owner
field_golden.notes: Single owner from new, purchased 2004; original box, warranty card and purchase receipt retained; full service history documented.
field_golden.groundTruthSource: Documented reference model (Rolex Submariner 16610); brand-archive-confirmable identity + full-set provenance. Expert tier per Method v21 §12 market positioning.

field_golden.name: Rolex Submariner 16610 — no papers
field_golden.groundTruthSource: Documented reference model, identity resolvable but no provenance documentation supplied — the honest Silver band.

field_golden.name: 'Daytona 6263' — super-clone (redial + non-genuine movement)
field_golden.groundTruthSource: Known super-clone construction: reprinted dial + movement inconsistent with the claimed calibre. The negative case (Acceptance B).
